# Toggle Group

Coordinates related toggles as a single- or multiple-selection control.

## Installation

Add Toggle Group from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/toggle-group
```

## Usage

Compose `ToggleGroupItem` components with unique `value` props inside `ToggleGroup`. Name the group with `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`, and give every icon-only item its own accessible label.

**Toggle group example**

```vue
<template>
  <ToggleGroup v-model="alignment" type="single" variant="outline" aria-label="Text alignment">
    <ToggleGroupItem value="left" aria-label="Align left"><AlignLeftIcon /></ToggleGroupItem>
    <ToggleGroupItem value="center" aria-label="Align center"><AlignCenterIcon /></ToggleGroupItem>
    <ToggleGroupItem value="right" aria-label="Align right"><AlignRightIcon /></ToggleGroupItem>
  </ToggleGroup>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { AlignCenterIcon, AlignLeftIcon, AlignRightIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem } from '@/Components/ToggleGroup'

const alignment = ref('left')
</script>
```

## Single and multiple selection

### Single

Set `type="single"` and bind one value when the options are mutually exclusive, such as text alignment. A toggle group may allow the active item to be turned off; when one answer must always remain selected, use Radio Group instead.

### Multiple

Set `type="multiple"` and bind an array when options may be combined, such as bold and italic formatting. Do not use multiple mode for choices that conflict with each other.

```vue
<ToggleGroup v-model="formatting" type="multiple" aria-label="Text formatting">
  <ToggleGroupItem value="bold" aria-label="Toggle bold">
    <BoldIcon />
  </ToggleGroupItem>
  <ToggleGroupItem value="italic" aria-label="Toggle italic">
    <ItalicIcon />
  </ToggleGroupItem>
</ToggleGroup>
```

## Controlled values

Use `v-model` when the selection affects other interface state. A single group binds one item value; a multiple group binds an array. Keep item values stable and do not reuse a display label as an identifier when that label may be translated or renamed.

```ts
const alignment = ref('left') // type="single"
const formatting = ref<string[]>(['bold']) // type="multiple"
```

## Variants, sizes, and disabled state

Set `variant` and `size` on the group to apply one consistent context to its items. Supported variants are default and outline; sizes are `sm`, `default`, and `lg`. Disable the group when every option is unavailable, or one item when only that choice is unavailable.

## Keyboard and orientation

Roving focus is enabled by default, with arrow keys moving among items and looping at the ends. Set `orientation` to match the visual layout so horizontal groups use left and right arrows and vertical groups use up and down arrows. Keep normal Tab order focused on the group rather than requiring a tab stop for every item.

## Choosing a selection control

Toggle Group performs actions and exposes pressed state. It is not a passive status display, segmented navigation, or a substitute for a form field with long answer labels.

> **Use Radio Group for a required form choice: [Radio Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/radio-group)**
>
> Radio Group is clearer when users must choose exactly one submitted answer and each option benefits from a visible label or description.

> **Use Tabs for switching views: [Tabs](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/tabs)**
>
> Tabs provide tab and panel semantics for switching visible sections. Use Toggle Group for modes and settings that remain applied to the current content.

Hosted documentation: [Toggle Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/toggle-group)
